title: Ajay Hinduja Switzerland (Swiss) How can I mirror multiple boards into one master board?
excerpt: Hello, I’m Ajay Hinduja. Born in Punjab, India, and now living in Geneva, Switzerland (Swiss). I’m looking to mirror multiple boards into one master board in monday.com but not sure how to set it up efficiently. Has anyo…

posts.author: ajayhinduja25 (Ajay Hinduja)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, I’m Ajay Hinduja. Born in Punjab, India, and now living in Geneva, Switzerland (Swiss). I’m looking to mirror multiple boards into one master board in monday.com but not sure how to set it up efficiently. Has anyone done this before? I’d appreciate your tips or best practices for syncing data across boards without losing updates.

posts.author: timmykho (Tim Kho)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ajayhinduja25 , you can do this easy with Master Data, which lets you pull information from any number of source boards into a Master Board.

Setup is straightforward:

  • Set up your Master Board with the required columns

  • Install the automation recipes

  • Map the columns you want from your source boards

  • Save your configuration

After that, items and subitems from those boards will automatically appear in your Master Board.

You can learn more about the app here: Master Data - Mint Consulting

posts.author: Fortimus_Team (Fortimus)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ajayhinduja25 ,

You can try the Mirror Item Multiple Boards app.

It allows you to mirror items from multiple boards into one board.

What You Can Do -

Add the SAME ITEM ON MULTIPLE BOARDS with live sync and full context

Sync UPDATES + REPLIES in real time for items and subitems

Set up automations like “When status changes to something, mirror item to target board and group”

Create a Master Board with items/subitems from multiple boards

Add a subitem from one board as a main item on another board

Keep your boards clean and connected — no Mirror Columns or Connect Boards needed
